intro: Ligusticum filisectum, also known as Chinese lovage or lovage of the mountains, is a plant species in the carrot family (Apiaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of China. The plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, and is often used to treat fever, headache, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Ligusticum filisectum include sesquiterpenes, coumarins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Ligusticum filisectum is commonly used in herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in combination with other herbs in traditional Chinese medicine formulas.
